message:
feat: Add Google Photos album mode with URL collection fallback

- Add gphotoUrls Set to capture handler for collecting album URLs
- Add Google Photos specific thumbnail selectors for album mode
- Implement URL collection fallback: if thumbnails not found, download
  all collected Google Photos CDN URLs using full-res =s0 modifier
- Complete Phase 3: Google Photos Support

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.1","title":"Implement normalize_dropbox_url() in bash","description":"## What

Add a function to transform Dropbox share URLs into direct download URLs.

## Implementation

```bash
normalize_dropbox_url() {
    local url="$1"
    
    # Remove existing dl= or raw= parameters
    url=$(echo "$url" | sed -E 's/[&?](dl|raw)=[01]//g')
    
    # Add raw=1 parameter
    if [[ "$url" == *"?"* ]]; then
        echo "${url}&raw=1"
    else
        echo "${url}?raw=1"
    fi
}
```

## Why raw=1 Instead of dl=1

| Parameter | Behavior |
|-----------|----------|
| `dl=0` | Web preview (default) |
| `dl=1` | Force download, `Content-Type: application/binary` |
| `raw=1` | Raw file, **correct MIME type** |

Using `raw=1` returns the correct `Content-Type` header, which helps with:
1. Content validation (we can check for image/* types)
2. Proper file handling by downstream tools
3. Browser compatibility (if URL is used elsewhere)

## Test Cases

```bash
# Standard share URL
normalize_dropbox_url "https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?rlkey=abc&dl=0"
# Expected: https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?rlkey=abc&raw=1

# URL without any dl parameter
normalize_dropbox_url "https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png"
# Expected: https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png?raw=1

# URL with raw=0 (someone manually changed it)
normalize_dropbox_url "https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png?raw=0"
# Expected: https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png?raw=1
```

## Edge Case: Multiple Query Parameters

The sed replacement handles both `?dl=0` and `&dl=0` cases. The `[&?]` pattern matches either.

Tests pass for all URL formats.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.1","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:20:54.928624792-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.2","title":"Implement Dropbox direct download (skip Playwright)","description":"## What

Add a fast path in the bash layer that downloads Dropbox files directly via curl, completely bypassing Playwright.

## Why This Matters

| Approach | Time | Memory | Reliability |
|----------|------|--------|-------------|
| Playwright | 5-15s | 300MB+ | Browser can fail |
| Direct curl | 1-3s | <10MB | Simple HTTP |

For Dropbox, Playwright provides WORSE quality (preview resolution). Direct download is the ONLY correct approach.

## Implementation

```bash
download_dropbox_direct() {
    local url="$1"
    local output_dir="$2"
    local quality="$3"
    local json_output="$4"
    
    # Normalize URL to raw format
    local raw_url
    raw_url=$(normalize_dropbox_url "$url")
    
    # Extract filename from URL
    local filename
    filename=$(basename "${url%%\?*}")
    
    # Generate output path with timestamp
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
    local output_path="${output_dir}/dropbox_${timestamp}.jpg"
    
    # Download with curl
    local http_code
    http_code=$(curl -sL -w "%{http_code}" "$raw_url" -o "$output_path")
    
    if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
        log_error "Download failed with HTTP $http_code"
        rm -f "$output_path"
        return $EXIT_NETWORK_ERROR
    fi
    
    # Validate content (check for HTML)
    local content_type
    content_type=$(file --mime-type -b "$output_path")
    if [[ "$content_type" == "text/html" ]]; then
        log_error "Received HTML instead of image. Link may be password-protected or expired."
        rm -f "$output_path"
        return $EXIT_AUTH_REQUIRED
    fi
    
    # Output result
    echo "$output_path"
    return $EXIT_SUCCESS
}
```

## Integration in Main Flow

```bash
main() {
    # ... parse args ...
    
    local platform
    platform=$(detect_platform "$URL")
    
    case "$platform" in
        dropbox)
            # Fast path: no Playwright needed
            download_dropbox_direct "$URL" "$OUTPUT_DIR" "$QUALITY" "$JSON_OUTPUT"
            exit $?
            ;;
        *)
            # Standard path: use Playwright extractor
            ensure_playwright
            node extractor.mjs "$URL" ...
            ;;
    esac
}
```

## Key Points

1. **Exit early**: Dropbox never reaches the Playwright code path
2. **Validate content**: Check file type after download
3. **Clean up on error**: Remove partial downloads
4. **Report clearly**: Use new exit codes (11 for auth, 10 for network)

Completely bypasses Playwright for faster downloads.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.2","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:21:16.36069909-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.2","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:27:34.226850452-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.2.3","title":"Add Dropbox error handling (password/expired)","description":"## What

Implement clear, actionable error messages for Dropbox failure modes.

## Error Scenarios

### 1. Password-Protected Link
Dropbox returns HTTP 200 with HTML containing password form.

**Detection:**
```bash
if grep -q "password" "$output_path" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Password required
fi
```

**Message:**
```
[giil] Error: This Dropbox link requires a password.
[giil] 
[giil] giil does not support password-protected shares.
[giil] Options:
[giil]   1. Ask the owner to create a password-free share
[giil]   2. Open the link in a browser and download manually
```

### 2. Expired Link
Dropbox returns HTTP 200 with HTML error page.

**Detection:**
```bash
if grep -qi "expired\|no longer available" "$output_path" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Link expired
fi
```

**Message:**
```
[giil] Error: This Dropbox link has expired or been revoked.
[giil] 
[giil] The file owner may have:
[giil]   - Deleted the file
[giil]   - Changed sharing settings
[giil]   - Set an expiration date that has passed
[giil] 
[giil] Ask the owner for a new share link.
```

### 3. File Not Found (404)
HTTP 404 status code.

**Message:**
```
[giil] Error: Dropbox file not found (404).
[giil] 
[giil] The link may be invalid or the file was deleted.
```

### 4. Rate Limited (429)
HTTP 429 status code.

**Message:**
```
[giil] Error: Too many requests to Dropbox.
[giil] 
[giil] Please wait a few minutes and try again.
```

## Implementation Pattern

```bash
handle_dropbox_error() {
    local http_code="$1"
    local output_path="$2"
    
    case "$http_code" in
        429)
            log_error "Too many requests to Dropbox. Please wait and try again."
            return $EXIT_NETWORK_ERROR
            ;;
        404)
            log_error "Dropbox file not found (404)."
            return $EXIT_NOT_FOUND
            ;;
        200)
            # Check if it's actually HTML (error page)
            if file --mime-type -b "$output_path" | grep -q "text/html"; then
                if grep -qi "password" "$output_path"; then
                    log_error "This Dropbox link requires a password."
                    log_error "giil does not support password-protected shares."
                    return $EXIT_AUTH_REQUIRED
                elif grep -qi "expired" "$output_path"; then
                    log_error "This Dropbox link has expired."
                    return $EXIT_NOT_FOUND
                else
                    log_error "Received HTML instead of image."
                    return $EXIT_AUTH_REQUIRED
                fi
            fi
            return $EXIT_SUCCESS
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Dropbox download failed with HTTP $http_code"
            return $EXIT_NETWORK_ERROR
            ;;
    esac
}
```

## Exit Code Mapping

| Scenario | Exit Code |
|----------|-----------|
| Password required | 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) |
| Link expired | 12 (NOT_FOUND) |
| File deleted | 12 (NOT_FOUND) |
| Rate limited | 10 (NETWORK_ERROR) |
| Unknown HTML | 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) |

-{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.3","title":"EPIC: Phase 3 - Google Photos Support","description":"## Overview

Add Google Photos support to giil. This requires Playwright for URL extraction but enables full-resolution downloads.

## Research Findings (Jan 2026)

### The Key Discovery

Google Photos CDN URLs include size modifiers:
- Default preview: `=w1042-h862-no` (1040×862)
- **Full resolution: `=s0`** (original size)

Simply appending `=s0` to the base URL returns the original image!

### Test Results
| Method | Resolution | Size | Status |
|--------|-----------|------|--------|
| URL extract + =s0 | 2760×2288 | 2.14 MB | ✅ Full |
| Playwright network | 1040×862 | 624 KB | ⚠️ Preview |

### URL Formats
```
Short link:  https://photos.app.goo.gl/{SHARE_ID}
Full link:   https://photos.google.com/share/{ALBUM_ID}?key={KEY}
```

### CDN Pattern
```
Base:    https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/{IMAGE_PATH}
Preview: https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/{IMAGE_PATH}=w1042-h862-no
Full:    https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/{IMAGE_PATH}=s0
```

## Strategy

1. **Load page with Playwright** (for URL extraction)
2. **Intercept network requests** to capture CDN base URL
3. **Extract base URL** (remove size modifier)
4. **Download directly** with `=s0` appended
5. **Validate content** (ensure it's an image, not HTML)

## Album Support

Google Photos albums show thumbnails that can be clicked to view full-size. For `--all` mode:

1. Collect all thumbnail URLs from network traffic
2. OR query for thumbnail elements (multiple selector strategies)
3. For each image: extract base URL, download with `=s0`

### Selector Strategies (Ordered by Stability)
```javascript
// Prefer semantic selectors
'[role="listitem"] a',
'a[aria-label*="photo" i]',

// Fall back to observed structures
'[data-latest-bg]',
'.RY3tic',
'[style*="background-image"]'
```

## Edge Cases

1. **Private albums**: Redirect to login → detect and report
2. **Single photo vs album**: Different DOM structure
3. **Video content**: Skip with warning or `--include-videos`

## 2025 API Changes

> From March 31, 2025, rclone can only download photos it uploaded.

This affects API-based tools but NOT giil (browser-based scraping of public web interface).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `giil "https://photos.app.goo.gl/..."` downloads at full resolution
- [ ] Short links (goo.gl) are followed to full URLs
- [ ] `--all` mode downloads all photos in album
- [ ] Clear error for private albums
- [ ] JSON output includes `platform: "gphotos"`


### CDN Pattern

```
Base:    https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/{IMAGE_PATH}
```

3. **Extract base URL** (remove size modifier)

Google Photos albums show thumbnails that can be clicked to view full-size. For `--all` mode:

1. Collect all thumbnail URLs from network traffic
2. OR query for thumbnail elements (multiple selector strategies)

### Selector Strategies (Ordered by Stability)
```javascript
// Prefer semantic selectors
'[role="listitem"] a',
```

This affects API-based tools but NOT giil (browser-based scraping of public web interface).

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.3.1","title":"Implement Google Photos CDN URL extraction","description":"## What

Capture Google Photos CDN URLs during page load and extract the base URL for full-resolution download.

## The Strategy

1. Install network interceptor BEFORE navigation
2. Capture all `lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/` URLs
3. Extract base URL (everything before the `=` size modifier)
4. Download full resolution using `{baseUrl}=s0`

## Implementation

```javascript
async function captureGooglePhotos(page, url) {
    const cdnUrls = new Set();
    
    // Install network interceptor BEFORE navigation
    page.on('response', async (response) => {
        const respUrl = response.url();
        
        // Capture Google Photos CDN URLs
        if (respUrl.includes('lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/')) {
            // Extract base URL (before size modifier)
            const baseUrl = respUrl.split('=')[0];
            cdnUrls.add(baseUrl);
        }
    });

    // Navigate to the share page
    await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' });

    // Wait for image to load (use element presence, not networkidle)
    await Promise.race([
        page.waitForSelector('[data-latest-bg], .RY3tic, img[src*="googleusercontent"]', 
            { timeout: 15000 }).catch(() => {}),
        page.waitForTimeout(5000)  // Fallback timeout
    ]);

    // Get the best URL (prefer largest if multiple)
    if (cdnUrls.size === 0) {
        throw new Error('No Google Photos CDN URL found');
    }

    // Use the first captured URL (for single photo)
    // For albums, we'd iterate through all
    const baseUrl = Array.from(cdnUrls)[0];
    const fullResUrl = `${baseUrl}=s0`;

    // Download full resolution directly
    const response = await fetch(fullResUrl);
    if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error(`Failed to download: HTTP ${response.status}`);
    }

    return {
        bytes: Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer()),
        mime: response.headers.get('content-type'),
        platform: 'gphotos',
        method: 'url-extraction',
        tier: 1,
        sourceUrl: fullResUrl
    };
}
```

## Size Modifier Reference

| Modifier | Result |
|----------|--------|
| `=w1042-h862-no` | Constrained to 1042×862 |
| `=w0` | Full width |
| `=s0` | **Original size** |
| `=w4000` | Capped at 4000px or original |

## Why `=s0`?

The `s` parameter specifies "size" without aspect ratio constraints. `=s0` means "size 0" which Google interprets as "original/unlimited".

## Error Handling

```javascript
// Detect private album (redirects to login)
if (page.url().includes('accounts.google.com')) {
    throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED: Album is private. Only public shares are supported.');
}

// Detect empty album
if (cdnUrls.size === 0 && await page.$('.empty-album-message')) {
    throw new Error('NOT_FOUND: Album appears to be empty.');
}
```

-{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.3.2","title":"Implement Google Photos album mode (--all)","description":"## What

Support downloading all photos from a shared Google Photos album using the `--all` flag.

## Strategy: Multi-Signal Approach

Album detection is fragile because Google's class names change. Use multiple signals:

### Signal 1: Network Traffic (Most Reliable)
Collect all unique `lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/` base URLs during page load.

```javascript
const albumUrls = new Set();

page.on('response', (response) => {
    const url = response.url();
    if (url.includes('lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/')) {
        albumUrls.add(url.split('=')[0]);  // Base URL
    }
});
```

### Signal 2: Semantic Selectors (More Stable)
```javascript
const thumbnailSelectors = [
    // Semantic (preferred)
    '[role="listitem"] a',
    'a[aria-label*="photo" i]',
    '[role="img"]',
    
    // Observed structures (fallback)
    '[data-latest-bg]',
    '.RY3tic',
    '[style*="background-image"]'
];
```

### Signal 3: Background Image URLs
```javascript
async function extractBackgroundUrls(page) {
    return await page.evaluate(() => {
        const urls = [];
        document.querySelectorAll('[style*="background-image"]').forEach(el => {
            const style = el.style.backgroundImage;
            const match = style.match(/url\("([^"]+lh3\.googleusercontent[^"]+)"\)/);
            if (match) urls.push(match[1].split('=')[0]);
        });
        return urls;
    });
}
```

## Implementation

```javascript
async function captureGooglePhotosAlbum(page, url) {
    const results = [];
    const collectedUrls = new Set();

    // Install interceptor for thumbnails
    page.on('response', (response) => {
        const respUrl = response.url();
        if (respUrl.includes('lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/')) {
            collectedUrls.add(respUrl.split('=')[0]);
        }
    });

    await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' });
    
    // Wait for thumbnails to load
    await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', { timeout: 15000 }).catch(() => {});

    // Scroll to load more thumbnails (if lazy-loaded)
    await scrollToLoadAll(page);

    // Combine with background image extraction
    const bgUrls = await extractBackgroundUrls(page);
    bgUrls.forEach(u => collectedUrls.add(u));

    // Download each unique URL with =s0
    let index = 1;
    for (const baseUrl of collectedUrls) {
        try {
            const fullUrl = `${baseUrl}=s0`;
            const response = await fetch(fullUrl);
            
            if (!response.ok) continue;

            results.push({
                bytes: Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer()),
                platform: 'gphotos',
                method: 'url-extraction',
                tier: 1,
                sourceUrl: fullUrl,
                index: index++
            });
        } catch (err) {
            console.error(`[giil] Photo ${index} failed: ${err.message}`);
            // Continue to next photo
        }
    }

    return results;
}

async function scrollToLoadAll(page, maxScrolls = 10) {
    for (let i = 0; i < maxScrolls; i++) {
        const prevHeight = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.scrollHeight);
        await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight));
        await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
        const newHeight = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.scrollHeight);
        if (newHeight === prevHeight) break;  // No more content
    }
}
```

## Output (--json mode)

Each photo outputs a separate JSON line:
```json
{"path": "gphotos_20260103_142245_001.jpg", "platform": "gphotos", "method": "url-extraction", ...}
{"path": "gphotos_20260103_142246_002.jpg", "platform": "gphotos", "method": "url-extraction", ...}
```

## Edge Cases

1. **Large albums**: Scroll pagination to load all thumbnails
2. **Videos in album**: Skip with warning (or `--include-videos`)
3. **Private album**: Detect login redirect, fail with clear message

+{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.3.2","title":"Implement Google Photos album mode (--all)","description":"## What

Support downloading all photos from a shared Google Photos album using the `--all` flag.

## Strategy: Multi-Signal Approach

Album detection is fragile because Google's class names change. Use multiple signals:

### Signal 1: Network Traffic (Most Reliable)
Collect all unique `lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/` base URLs during page load.

```javascript
const albumUrls = new Set();

page.on('response', (response) => {
    const url = response.url();
    if (url.includes('lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/')) {
        albumUrls.add(url.split('=')[0]);  // Base URL
    }
});
```

### Signal 2: Semantic Selectors (More Stable)
```javascript
const thumbnailSelectors = [
    // Semantic (preferred)
    '[role="listitem"] a',
    'a[aria-label*="photo" i]',
    '[role="img"]',
    
    // Observed structures (fallback)
    '[data-latest-bg]',
    '.RY3tic',
    '[style*="background-image"]'
];
```

### Signal 3: Background Image URLs
```javascript
async function extractBackgroundUrls(page) {
    return await page.evaluate(() => {
        const urls = [];
        document.querySelectorAll('[style*="background-image"]').forEach(el => {
            const style = el.style.backgroundImage;
            const match = style.match(/url\("([^"]+lh3\.googleusercontent[^"]+)"\)/);
            if (match) urls.push(match[1].split('=')[0]);
        });
        return urls;
    });
}
```

## Implementation

```javascript
async function captureGooglePhotosAlbum(page, url) {
    const results = [];
    const collectedUrls = new Set();

    // Install interceptor for thumbnails
    page.on('response', (response) => {
        const respUrl = response.url();
        if (respUrl.includes('lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/')) {
            collectedUrls.add(respUrl.split('=')[0]);
        }
    });

    await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' });
    
    // Wait for thumbnails to load
    await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', { timeout: 15000 }).catch(() => {});

    // Scroll to load more thumbnails (if lazy-loaded)
    await scrollToLoadAll(page);

    // Combine with background image extraction
    const bgUrls = await extractBackgroundUrls(page);
    bgUrls.forEach(u => collectedUrls.add(u));

    // Download each unique URL with =s0
    let index = 1;
    for (const baseUrl of collectedUrls) {
        try {
            const fullUrl = `${baseUrl}=s0`;
            const response = await fetch(fullUrl);
            
            if (!response.ok) continue;

            results.push({
                bytes: Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer()),
                platform: 'gphotos',
                method: 'url-extraction',
                tier: 1,
                sourceUrl: fullUrl,
                index: index++
            });
        } catch (err) {
            console.error(`[giil] Photo ${index} failed: ${err.message}`);
            // Continue to next photo
        }
    }

    return results;
}

async function scrollToLoadAll(page, maxScrolls = 10) {
    for (let i = 0; i < maxScrolls; i++) {
        const prevHeight = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.scrollHeight);
        await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight));
        await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
        const newHeight = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.scrollHeight);
        if (newHeight === prevHeight) break;  // No more content
    }
}
```

## Output (--json mode)

Each photo outputs a separate JSON line:
```json
{"path": "gphotos_20260103_142245_001.jpg", "platform": "gphotos", "method": "url-extraction", ...}
{"path": "gphotos_20260103_142246_002.jpg", "platform": "gphotos", "method": "url-extraction", ...}
```

## Edge Cases

1. **Large albums**: Scroll pagination to load all thumbnails
2. **Videos in album**: Skip with warning (or `--include-videos`)
3. **Private album**: Detect login redirect, fail with clear message

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.4","title":"EPIC: Phase 4 - Google Drive Support (Experimental)","description":"## Overview

Add Google Drive support to giil. This is the most complex platform due to 2024 API changes and strict sharing requirements.

**CRITICAL: Ship as [experimental] in v3.0**

## Why Experimental

1. **2024 API changes**: Direct download URLs (`/uc?export=download`) now frequently return 403
2. **Strict sharing**: Only works with "Anyone with the link" permission
3. **Complex failure modes**: Many ways to fail, all return HTTP 200 with HTML
4. **Unreliable direct URLs**: Thumbnail endpoints may return lower resolution

## Research Findings (Jan 2026)

### Test Results (Private File)
```
All methods redirected to Google login:
- /uc?export=view     → accounts.google.com (HTML)
- /uc?export=download → accounts.google.com (HTML)
- /thumbnail?sz=w2000 → accounts.google.com (HTML)
- Playwright          → accounts.google.com (HTML)

Conclusion: File requires "Anyone with the link" sharing
```

### URL Formats
```
View:      https://drive.google.com/file/d/{FILE_ID}/view
Open:      https://drive.google.com/open?id={FILE_ID}
Download:  https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id={FILE_ID}
Thumbnail: https://drive.google.com/thumbnail?id={FILE_ID}&sz=w{SIZE}
```

### File ID Extraction
```javascript
const patterns = [
    /\/file\/d\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/,
    /\/open\?id=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/,
    /[?&]id=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/
];
```

## Multi-Tier Strategy

1. **Check if public**: Navigate to view URL, detect login redirect
2. **Try download button**: Click aria-label="Download" if visible
3. **Try direct download**: `/uc?export=download&id={fileId}`
4. **Try thumbnail**: `/thumbnail?id={fileId}&sz=w4000` (may be lower res)
5. **Screenshot fallback**: Element or viewport screenshot

## Required User Action

For Drive to work, file owner MUST:
1. Right-click file → Share → General access
2. Change "Restricted" to "Anyone with the link"
3. Click Done

## Edge Cases

1. **Large files (>100MB)**: Virus scan warning bypass
2. **Google Docs/Sheets**: Not images, detect and skip
3. **Quota exceeded**: Rate limiting, implement backoff

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Public images download correctly
- [ ] Private files show clear "not shared" error with remediation steps
- [ ] Help text marks Drive as [experimental]
- [ ] JSON output includes `platform: "gdrive"`

## Non-Goals (v3.0)

- No authenticated mode (user login)
- No cookie persistence
- No bypassing login/CAPTCHA

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.4.1","title":"Implement Google Drive file ID extraction","description":"## What

Extract the file ID from various Google Drive URL formats.

## Implementation

```javascript
function extractGoogleDriveFileId(url) {
    const patterns = [
        /\/file\/d\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/,      // /file/d/{id}/view
        /\/open\?id=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/,       // /open?id={id}
        /[?&]id=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/            // ?id={id} or &id={id}
    ];

    for (const pattern of patterns) {
        const match = url.match(pattern);
        if (match) return match[1];
    }
    return null;
}
```

## URL Format Examples

| Input URL | Extracted ID |
|-----------|--------------|
| `https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ABC123xyz/view` | `1ABC123xyz` |
| `https://drive.google.com/open?id=1ABC123xyz` | `1ABC123xyz` |
| `https://drive.google.com/uc?id=1ABC123xyz&export=download` | `1ABC123xyz` |

## Error Handling

```javascript
const fileId = extractGoogleDriveFileId(url);
if (!fileId) {
    throw new Error('USAGE_ERROR: Could not extract file ID from Google Drive URL');
}
```

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.4.2","title":"Implement Google Drive auth detection","description":"## What

Detect when a Google Drive file requires authentication and provide clear, actionable error messages.

## Detection Strategy

### 1. Login Redirect Detection
```javascript
async function checkDriveAuth(page, fileId) {
    const viewUrl = `https://drive.google.com/file/d/${fileId}/view`;
    await page.goto(viewUrl, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 15000 });

    // Check for login redirect
    const currentUrl = page.url();
    if (currentUrl.includes('accounts.google.com')) {
        throw new DriveAuthError('File requires authentication. Not publicly shared.');
    }

    // Check for "request access" page
    const requestAccessBtn = await page.$('button:has-text("Request access")');
    if (requestAccessBtn) {
        throw new DriveAuthError('File is restricted. Owner must grant access.');
    }
}
```

### 2. API Response Detection
```javascript
// Some Drive URLs return 200 with HTML error page
async function validateDriveResponse(buffer) {
    const text = buffer.toString('utf8', 0, 1000);
    
    if (text.includes('accounts.google.com/ServiceLogin')) {
        throw new DriveAuthError('Login required');
    }
    
    if (text.includes('Request access') || text.includes('You need permission')) {
        throw new DriveAuthError('Access denied');
    }
    
    if (text.includes('Sorry, the file you have requested does not exist')) {
        throw new DriveNotFoundError('File not found or deleted');
    }
}
```

## Error Messages

### Not Publicly Shared
```
[giil] Error: Google Drive file is not publicly shared
[giil]
[giil] The file owner must:
[giil]   1. Right-click the file in Google Drive
[giil]   2. Click "Share" → "General access"
[giil]   3. Change "Restricted" to "Anyone with the link"
[giil]   4. Click "Done"
[giil]
[giil] Then try again with the same URL.
```

### File Not Found
```
[giil] Error: Google Drive file not found
[giil]
[giil] The file may have been:
[giil]   - Deleted by the owner
[giil]   - Moved to a different location
[giil]   - The URL may be incorrect
```

## Exit Code Mapping

| Scenario | Exit Code |
|----------|-----------|
| Login redirect | 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) |
| Request access page | 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) |
| File not found | 12 (NOT_FOUND) |
| Download quota | 10 (NETWORK_ERROR) |
